sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.rendered_content:
This comparison evaluates NVDA, ASML, TSM, AMD across their locked annual filing periods, examining revenue scale, profitability, leverage, and cash generation to surface relative positioning within the peer set.

NVDA reports $216B in revenue (65.5% YoY) and $120B in net income for period 2026-01-25. Key profile markers include net margin of 55.6%, debt-to-equity of 0.06x, free cash flow of $96.7B.

ASML reports $38.4B in revenue (30.7% YoY) and $11.3B in net income for period 2025-12-31. Key profile markers include net margin of 29.4%, debt-to-equity of 0.22x, free cash flow of $13.0B.

TSM reports $88.3B in revenue (25.0% YoY) and $35.3B in net income for period 2024-12-31. Key profile markers include net margin of 40.0%, free cash flow of $26.5B.

AMD reports $34.6B in revenue (34.3% YoY) and $4.33B in net income for period 2025-12-27. Key profile markers include net margin of 12.5%, debt-to-equity of 0.05x, free cash flow of $6.74B.

NVDA leads the peer set in annual revenue, anchoring the scale dimension of the comparison.

sections.title: Relative Strengths
sections.rendered_content:
### NVDA — What Stands Out
- Gross margin of 71.1% indicates strong pricing power.

- ROE of 101.5% indicates efficient use of shareholder capital.
- Revenue is up 65.5% year over year, showing strong top-line momentum.
- Current ratio of 3.91x indicates solid liquidity.
### ASML — What Stands Out
- Gross margin of 52.8% indicates strong pricing power.

- ROE of 53.5% indicates efficient use of shareholder capital.
- Revenue is up 30.7% year over year, showing strong top-line momentum.
### TSM — What Stands Out
- Gross margin of 56.1% indicates strong pricing power.

- ROE of 29.0% indicates efficient use of shareholder capital.
- Revenue is up 25.0% year over year, showing strong top-line momentum.
- Current ratio of 2.36x indicates solid liquidity.
### AMD — What Stands Out
- Revenue is up 34.3% year over year, showing strong top-line momentum.

- Current ratio of 2.85x indicates solid liquidity.

sections.title: Risk Comparison
sections.rendered_content:
### Watch Items
- Relative risk is assessed only on surfaced, period-locked peer metrics and does not force false comparability.

- **TSM:** No major quantitative red flag is active in the current annual snapshot.

sections.title: Analyst Notes
sections.rendered_content:
### Analyst Follow-Up
- Peer conclusions should be read through the locked annual basis, surfaced metric coverage, and explicit comparison-governance policy.

- **NVDA:** 4 strength signals and 6 active risk signals in the locked annual basis.
- **ASML:** 3 strength signals and 1 active risk signals in the locked annual basis.
- **TSM:** 4 strength signals and 0 active risk signals in the locked annual basis.
- **AMD:** 2 strength signals and 4 active risk signals in the locked annual basis.
- Prioritize next-pass work on margin durability, balance-sheet flexibility, and cash conversion differentials across the peer set.
